HannahW wrote:
[*]Votes and Hangs
This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

  • Votes must be in this format: Vote: [player username] to be counted.
  • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
  • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
  • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjayy., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
  • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
  • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
  • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
  • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please notify the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
  • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
  • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
  • If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, No Hang will occur.
  • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
  • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
  • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
  • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.


[*]Activity
This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
  • Each day phase will be 336 hours (14 days), and each night phase will be 72 hours (3 days).
  • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
  • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
  • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
  • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline.
  • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
  • V/LAs should be shorter than a week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options.
  • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
  • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
  • Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
  • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
  • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
  • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
  • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. This will not count as an official prod.
  • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.

- Conduct Guidelines -
This tab summarizes important conduct guidelines.

Important note: If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or even help you find a better way to cope. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM.
  1. Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  2. Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  3. Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.
  4. Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  5. You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  6. First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.

  • Sportsmanship
    This tab covers concepts conducive to healthy gameplay and steps moderators may take to intervene if things go wrong.

    Most players will not encounter the situations detailed below nor be warned even if they do not observe these guidelines.

    • Do not gamethrow.

    This includes: hard claiming mafia for no reason, outing your teammates, posting screenshots of the Mafia QT, hanging a conftown as town
    This does not include: giving up, strategic suiciding, lolcatting, self-voting

    You should play towards your wincondition to the best of your ability. Intentionally sabotaging the game for any reason - such as trolling, a personal vendetta, or frustration - is prohibited. Moderator action will likely be severe, ranging from a modkill to a forum ban. Note that only intentionally playing against your wincon and hurting your teammates is banned - you are otherwise allowed to play however you like: giving up, being unhelpful, or making mistakes in good faith will not prompt intervention.

    Important note: According to decisions made by forum moderators detailed in this post, anything that appears to be gamethrowing - real or fake - is prohibited. Any serious statement along the lines of "I am actually mafia and/or my partner is [player]" that appears to be made with the intent to gamethrow will result in an immediate modkill, even if it is false. Please avoid this type of situation entirely until you have enough experience to navigate the fine line between lolcatting and gamethrowing.

    • Discussing player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other moderator interactions is discouraged.

    This includes: "[player] was probably force replaced for PMing their mafia partner"
    This does not include: discussing lurking or player activity in reference to their alignment

    Seriously discussing a player's alignment based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, or other essential moderator communications is highly discouraged. Speculation, jokes, or hot takes about such events are okay, but if a serious discussion occurs, the moderators may step in and ask participants to stop. Repeatedly refusing to listen to the moderators will result in a warning.

    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.

    This includes: using encrypted files only certain players possess the key to, using cryptosystems, using encryption programs to hide messages in posts
    This does not include: breadcrumbs, manually encoded/decoded ciphers, public riddles, brief conversations in non-English languages

    Unless private daychat is specifically enabled in the game, attempting to communicate with other players privately is strictly prohibited. All game-related information should be visible and reasonably available to be accessed and/or analyzed by all players. If you wish to establish a secret method of daytime communication with your teammates, it may be a good idea to run your idea by the moderator team. Enforcement will likely range from a warning or a request to cease using the method in question.

    • Avoiding referencing OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or creating OGI situations that could be construed as alignment indicative is recommended.

    This includes: adding all game players to an "unrelated" PM, "[player] is active in their other games, but not this one. Are they lurking as maf?" or "[player] and [player] have been acting unusually friendly in [this unrelated forum], I bet they're actually the scumteam."
    This does not include: mentioning real life, online interactions, other unrelated comments as long as they are alignment agnostic

    OGI (Out of Game Information) is anything that happens outside of the game thread and it is only problematic when used alignment-indicatively. Remember, a) do not publicly discuss a read based on OGI, and b) avoid situations outside the game thread that could cause other players to suspect your alignment, town or mafia. Everything else is fine, and mentioning occurrences outside the thread in the game thread is also fine, but it must be alignment agnostic. This is to preserve game integrity by minimizing outside influences. The moderators may intervene to stop an OGI discussion or incident. Repeated failure to listen to the moderators will result in a warning.

  • Etiquette
    This tab covers player etiquette, warning limits, and discouraged conduct.

    • Do not make personal attacks.
    • Do not spam.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    Please note the types of posts implicitly discouraged by the Mafia! community. If certain criteria are met, players are liable to be warned for these posts. The following section should have little on normal player behavior and has been created to better define "do not abuse loopholes" and to encourage healthier gameplay.

    Discouraged Player Conduct
    This tab identifies posts that may result in a warning.

    This section may be updated periodically. If this happens during a game, a notice will be posted in the bottom of the OP.

    • Posts with formatting that mimics the text indicating that a post has been edited
    • Posts that are blank or otherwise contain no content
    • Unsolicited advertising
    • Multiple posts with identical content
    • Posts containing large blocks of unoriginal text
    • Extended or in-depth communication in code or non-English languages

    These posts typically will not receive a warning unless one or more of the following criteria are met:

    1. The player has a history of warnings and/or disruptive behavior.
    2. The posts are excessive in number, scale, or objectionable content.
    3. The posts have caused notably negative reactions from other players.

    The majority of players should rarely, if ever, have trouble with these guidelines. This is simply to clarify the anti-loophole rule and to reduce deliberately provoking behaviors that were not explicitly forbidden by previous rulesets.

  • Moderation Policies
    This tab clarifies how the moderators will handle various notable events.

    • Role Randomization

    When randomizing roles for the game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation. Both newbie/newbie and SE/SE scumteams are thus possible.

    Current RNG Process (Matrix6)
    Once the playerlist has been finalized, the setup will be randomized by the Random Integer Generator:



    The numbers correspond to the order in which the 6 possible setups are listed in the Setup tab.

    Once a setup has been chosen, the roles will be listed alphabetically in the following order: VTs, Town PRs, Mafia Goon(s), Mafia PR. This list will be randomized by the List Randomizer and merged with the playerlist, which is ordered by descending rank, then by alphabetical order. The game's ordered playerlist is in the Alive/Dead tabs.


    • Tiebreaking

    If there is a tie at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made in Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made in Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This avoids tiebreaking via RNG while preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting.

    • Votes cast during deadline

    If votes or unvotes are cast near the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the officially logged post times. These can be found by locating the relevant post at aops.com/community/u[player ID] and referencing its upper-right corner.

    Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes logged as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not count.

    • Game-advancing Posts

    A "game-advancing post" is anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. Most posts other than "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks.

    • Postbans

    Due to clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, moderators cannot proxy posts from postbanned players. If a player is postbanned, they will be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation if the postban is less than a week in length.

    Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help.

    You may contact the moderators through discord at unclunk#0749 or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS.

    • Outside Consulting

    If needed, moderators may contact a forum or other respected mod for help resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity. If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the moderators' course of action, you may request the moderators to consult a second opinion, if they have not done so already.

    • Out Of Thread

    Due to clarifications by fdas, strictly mentioning procedural aspects of this game, such as game number, rules, player names, will not be OOT. However anything beyond that, such as flavor, gamestate, gameplays, will be OOT.

  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • Hannahw and mathjay are the moderators of Mafia Game 227.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green (#00f752), Activity Check blue (#005dff), mathjay's blue (#4a86e8), HannahW's blue (#0b5394).
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OP changelog.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The Version History tab lists important changes.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

Setup
This game is based on the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
  • Setup 1: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 2: 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 3: 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup A: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup B: 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup C: 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified:
  • This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then.
  • Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock on the same night.
  • Cop results are sane.
  • Mafia have daychat.
Role PMs
Incarcerated Janitor (Vanilla Townie) wrote:
Hello, [player]! You are an Incarcerated Janitor (Vanilla Townie).

For some reason or another, you're in jail. You might have extorted someone, scammed a few others, or taken a bribe, but you never dreamed of reaching such heights--literally. Because you're a prisoner, a nonindependent human being, the prison gets to hire you out, so here you are 4000 feet in the air with a load of cleaning supplies: free (or so you would like) from the jail at last. You're not exactly sure how this works, so although you haven't seen another soul and are lodged with just two strange persons overseeing you, you've decided not to be too uneasy… After all, you have a neat job description now? But all that aside, the squalish wealth you're cleaning up is dizzying: you might be able to sneak a diamond or two here and there if you can get out without being killed by some psychopath. You might even try to escape for good. Your steel bucket is good for knocking people out…

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote. You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Laboratory Sanitation Expert (Town Doctor) wrote:
Hello, [player]! You are the Laboratory Sanitation Expert (Town Doctor).

You're utterly disgusted that you're in jail. You're even more utterly disgusted that you're now a janitor. Who are you really? You're an expert and real and meaningful cleaning. That's right. Before you were jailed for unethical experimentation on human fetuses, you were a prominent, PhD-ed, world renowned expert on antibacterial solutions, especially in relation to biological tissue. Daily you lament how far you've fallen, but you still have some of your flabbergastingly expensive antibacterial, antiviral, activated-charcoal, and hemoglobin-reactionary cauterization cleaner solution with you from the lab. You're willing, if that's what it takes, to use this to save people, because you're definitely not going to die cleaning up after luxury. If you must die, you're determined to die in luxury--whatever it takes.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Artifact Liberation Specialist (Town Jailkeeper) wrote:

6 is an awfully young age to pick a lock, but your mom put a box of gourmet chocolates in her desk drawer and locked it. You’ve come a long way since picking 3-pin locks with a couple paperclips as a grade schooler: you’re now proficient in everything from electronic blast doors to old safes, and the only reason you were stuck in jail were the guards. Anything inanimate is no problem at all for you. People are just nosy sometimes. Otherwise, however, you could unlock something as mundane as a door in your sleep--but, now, locking things? Hello, [player]! You are a Normal Person (Mafia Goon).

You believe that ending people's lives is right; it is a moral duty. No, it's not Darwinism or overpopulation, you explain gently to others as their horrid life finally departs, and as they lay at rest in your arms, you softly ask them, do they now understand? Of course, they can't understand. They're gone, and that's it: life is nothing. The pain they've lived is nothing. The false and fleeting pleasure they were slave to is nothing. There is no optimism in nihilism. Just nothing. Now they acknowledge it too. And you bid them farewell.

But what dictates what's right? What if life isn't worth nothing? What if it doesn't just end? What if life is something? Those, however, are not your questions. You don't ask questions. You live your truth, killing as many as you can before you must, too, become to others what you've been all along: nothing.

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in this QuickTopic while you are alive. One of you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Also Very Normal Person (Mafia Roleblocker) wrote:
Hello, [player]! You are a Also Normal Person (Mafia Roleblocker).

You've been partners with [player] ([role]) for years now, both before jail and in jail. It’s quite rare nowadays to find one whose philosophy and beliefs correlate exactly with yours, but maybe it’s because you both had similar backgrounds… but your backstory isn’t the most pleasant subject. Lots of smoke and muttered nonsense.
At any rate, you keep the keys to your fellow incarcerated janitors’ rooms, salvaged from the corpses of the Unpaid Interns. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then lock that person in with an external padlock for that night, preventing their night actions, if any, from resolving.

You may discuss with your partner in this QuickTopic while you are alive. One of you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. You can simultaneously perform both the factional kill and your roleblock. You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. The game thread is here. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[quote=HannahW]
[*][hide=Votes and Hangs]
[b]This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.[/b]


[list][*]Votes must be in this format: [b]Vote: [player username][/b] to be counted.
[*]Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly.[u] Do not use nicknames[/u]. 
[*]For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical. 
[*]For example, both [b]Vote: mathjay[/b] and [b]Vote: mAtHjAy[/b] are valid votes, but [b]vote: mathjay[/b], [b]Vote: jay[/b], [b]Vote: mathjayy.[/b], [b]Vote:mathjay[/b], Vote: [b]mathjay[/b], and [b][i]Vote: mathjay[/i][/b] are invalid. 
[*]Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
[*]Vote to hang no one by posting [b]Vote: No Hang[/b].
[*]Unvote by posting [b]Unvote[/b]. You do not have to unvote before voting again. 
[*]Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please notify the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
[*]Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged. 
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, No Hang will occur.
[*]Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
[*]Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will be 336 hours (14 days), and each night phase will be 72 hours (3 days). 
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than a week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. This will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide][/list]
[hide=- Conduct Guidelines -]
[b]This tab summarizes important conduct guidelines.[/b]

[b]Important note:[/b] If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or even help you find a better way to cope. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM. 

[list=1][*]Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition. 
[*]Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
[*]Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games. 
[*]Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read. 
[*]You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves. 
[*]First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.[/list][/hide]

[list][*][hide=Sportsmanship]
[b]This tab covers concepts conducive to healthy gameplay and steps moderators may take to intervene if things go wrong.[/b] 

Most players will not encounter the situations detailed below nor be warned even if they do not observe these guidelines. 


[list][*]Do not gamethrow.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] hard claiming mafia for no reason, outing your teammates, posting screenshots of the Mafia QT, hanging a conftown as town
[b]This does not include:[/b] giving up, strategic suiciding, lolcatting, self-voting

You should play towards your wincondition to the best of your ability. Intentionally sabotaging the game for any reason - such as trolling, a personal vendetta, or frustration - is prohibited. Moderator action will likely be severe, ranging from a modkill to a forum ban. Note that only intentionally playing against your wincon and hurting your teammates is banned - you are otherwise allowed to play however you like: giving up, being unhelpful, or making mistakes in good faith will not prompt intervention. 

[b]Important note:[/b] According to decisions made by forum moderators [url=http://bit.ly/AMafgt]detailed in this post[/url], anything that appears to be gamethrowing - real or fake - is prohibited. Any serious statement along the lines of "I am actually mafia and/or my partner is [player]" that appears to be made with the intent to gamethrow will result in an immediate modkill, even if it is false. Please avoid this type of situation entirely until you have enough experience to navigate the fine line between lolcatting and gamethrowing. 


[list][*]Discussing player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other moderator interactions is discouraged.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] "[player] was probably force replaced for PMing their mafia partner"
[b]This does not include:[/b] discussing lurking or player activity in reference to their alignment

Seriously discussing a player's alignment based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, or other essential moderator communications is highly discouraged. Speculation, jokes, or hot takes about such events are okay, but if a serious discussion occurs, the moderators may step in and ask participants to stop. Repeatedly refusing to listen to the moderators will result in a warning. 


[list][*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] using encrypted files only certain players possess the key to, using cryptosystems, using encryption programs to hide messages in posts
[b]This does not include:[/b] breadcrumbs, manually encoded/decoded ciphers, public riddles, brief conversations in non-English languages

Unless private daychat is specifically enabled in the game, attempting to communicate with other players privately is strictly prohibited. All game-related information should be visible and reasonably available to be accessed and/or analyzed by all players. If you wish to establish a secret method of daytime communication with your teammates, it may be a good idea to run your idea by the moderator team. Enforcement will likely range from a warning or a request to cease using the method in question. 


[list][*]Avoiding referencing OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or creating OGI situations that could be construed as alignment indicative is recommended.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] adding all game players to an "unrelated" PM, "[player] is active in their other games, but not this one. Are they lurking as maf?" or "[player] and [player] have been acting unusually friendly in [this unrelated forum], I bet they're actually the scumteam."
[b]This does not include:[/b] mentioning real life, online interactions, other unrelated comments as long as they are alignment agnostic

OGI (Out of Game Information) is anything that happens outside of the game thread and it is only problematic when used alignment-indicatively. Remember, a) do not publicly discuss a read based on OGI, and b) avoid situations outside the game thread that could cause other players to suspect your alignment, town or mafia. Everything else is fine, and mentioning occurrences outside the thread in the game thread is also fine, but it must be alignment agnostic. This is to preserve game integrity by minimizing outside influences. The moderators may intervene to stop an OGI discussion or incident. Repeated failure to listen to the moderators will result in a warning.[/hide]

[*][hide=Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers player etiquette, warning limits, and discouraged conduct.[/b]


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks.
[*]Do not spam. 
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning. 
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines. 
[*]Be mature and use common sense. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

Please note the types of posts implicitly discouraged by the Mafia! community. If certain criteria are met, players are liable to be warned for these posts. The following section should have little on normal player behavior and has been created to better define "do not abuse loopholes" and to encourage healthier gameplay. 

[hide=Discouraged Player Conduct]
[b]This tab identifies posts that may result in a warning.[/b]

[i]This section may be updated periodically. If this happens during a game, a notice will be posted in the bottom of the OP.[/i]


[list][*]Posts with formatting that mimics the text indicating that a post has been edited
[*]Posts that are blank or otherwise contain no content
[*]Unsolicited advertising
[*]Multiple posts with identical content
[*]Posts containing large blocks of unoriginal text
[*]Extended or in-depth communication in code or non-English languages[/list]

These posts typically will not receive a warning unless one or more of the following criteria are met: 


[list=1][*]The player has a history of warnings and/or disruptive behavior.
[*]The posts are excessive in number, scale, or objectionable content.
[*]The posts have caused notably negative reactions from other players.[/list]

The majority of players should rarely, if ever, have trouble with these guidelines. This is simply to clarify the anti-loophole rule and to reduce deliberately provoking behaviors that were not explicitly forbidden by previous rulesets.[/hide][/hide]

[*][hide=Moderation Policies]
[b]This tab clarifies how the moderators will handle various notable events.[/b]


[list][*]Role Randomization[/list]

When randomizing roles for the game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation. Both newbie/newbie and SE/SE scumteams are thus possible. 

[hide=Current RNG Process (Matrix6)]
Once the playerlist has been finalized, the setup will be randomized by the [url=https://www.random.org/integers/]Random Integer Generator[/url]: 

[img]http://bit.ly/M6rand[/img]

The numbers correspond to the order in which the 6 possible setups are listed in the Setup tab. 

Once a setup has been chosen, the roles will be listed alphabetically in the following order: VTs, Town PRs, Mafia Goon(s), Mafia PR. This list will be randomized by the [url=https://www.random.org/lists/]List Randomizer[/url] and merged with the playerlist, which is ordered by descending rank, then by alphabetical order. The game's ordered playerlist is in the Alive/Dead tabs.[/hide]


[list][*]Tiebreaking[/list]

If there is a tie at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made in Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made in Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This avoids tiebreaking via RNG while preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting. 


[list][*]Votes cast during deadline[/list]

If votes or unvotes are cast near the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the officially logged post times. These can be found by locating the relevant post at aops.com/community/u[player ID] and referencing its upper-right corner. 

Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes logged as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not count. 


[list][*]Game-advancing Posts[/list]

A "game-advancing post" is anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. Most posts other than "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks. 


[list][*]Postbans[/list]

Due to clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, moderators cannot proxy posts from postbanned players. If a player is postbanned, they will be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation if the postban is less than a week in length. 

Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help. 

You may contact the moderators through discord at [b]unclunk#0749[/b] or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS. 


[list][*]Outside Consulting[/list]

If needed, moderators may contact a forum or other respected mod for help resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity. If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the moderators' course of action, you may request the moderators to consult a second opinion, if they have not done so already.


[list][*]Out Of Thread[/list]

Due to [url=http://bit.ly/fmooc]clarifications[/url] by fdas, strictly mentioning procedural aspects of this game, such as game number, rules, player names, will not be OOT. However anything beyond that, such as flavor, gamestate, gameplays, will be OOT.[/hide]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]Hannahw and mathjay are the moderators of Mafia Game 227. 
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate. 
[*][color=#FF0000]Red text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
[*][color=#0000FF]Blue text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*][color=#00FF00]Green text[/color] is used for town flavor-related text.
[*]Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*]Current custom shades are: Town flavor green ([color=#00f752]#00f752[/color]), Activity Check blue ([color=#005dff]#005dff[/color]), mathjay's blue ([color=#4a86e8]#4a86e8[/color]), HannahW's blue ([color=#0b5394]#0b5394[/color]).
[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OP changelog. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The Version History tab lists important changes. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game. [/list]

[hide=Setup]This game is based on the [url=http://bit.ly/m6msw]Matrix6[/url] setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
[list][*][b]Setup 1:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 2:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup 3:[/b] 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup A:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup B:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup C:[/b] 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons[/list]
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified: 
[list][*]This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then. 
[*]Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock on the same night.
[*]Cop results are sane.
[*]Mafia have daychat.[/list]
[hide=Role PMs][quote="Incarcerated Janitor (Vanilla Townie)"]
Hello, [player]! You are an [color=#00f752]Incarcerated Janitor (Vanilla Townie)[/color]. 

For some reason or another, you're in jail. You might have extorted someone, scammed a few others, or taken a bribe, but you never dreamed of reaching such heights--literally. Because you're a prisoner, a nonindependent human being, the prison gets to hire you out, so here you are 4000 feet in the air with a load of cleaning supplies: free (or so you would like) from the jail at last. You're not exactly sure how this works, so although you haven't seen another soul and are lodged with just two strange persons overseeing you, you've decided not to be too uneasy… After all, you have a neat job description now? But all that aside, the squalish wealth you're cleaning up is dizzying: you might be able to sneak a diamond or two here and there if you can get out without being killed by some psychopath. You might even try to escape for good. Your steel bucket is good for knocking people out… 

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote. You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Laboratory Sanitation Expert (Town Doctor)"]
Hello, [player]! You are the [color=#00f752]Laboratory Sanitation Expert (Town Doctor)[/color]. 

You're utterly disgusted that you're in jail. You're even more utterly disgusted that you're now a janitor. Who are you [i]really[/i]? You're an expert and [i]real and meaningful[/i] cleaning. That's right. Before you were jailed for unethical experimentation on human fetuses, you were a prominent, PhD-ed, world renowned expert on antibacterial solutions, especially in relation to biological tissue. Daily you lament how far you've fallen, but you still have some of your flabbergastingly expensive antibacterial, antiviral, activated-charcoal, and hemoglobin-reactionary cauterization cleaner solution with you from the lab. You're willing, if that's what it takes, to use this to save people, because you're definitely not going to die cleaning up after luxury. If you must die, you're determined to die in luxury--whatever it takes.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then protect them for the night and prevent them from dying. You can save yourself, but you cannot do this two nights in a row. You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Disciple of Crime Scene Investigation (Town Tracker)"]
Hello, [player]! You are the [color=#00f752]Disciple of Crime Scene Investigation (Town Tracker)[/color]. 

You're pretty sure you're the only janitor who actually was a janitor before all these prison labor program shenanigans. While the others are grumbling, you're nostalgic: nobody ever notices cleaning staff--it’s one of the things that have always intrigued you. It's also what booked you a 5-year trip to the penitentiary. That part you still blame on bad security and an overly enticing opportunity… Anyway, you just happened to be prepared for this very scenario, being the resourceful janitor that you are. You may not have electronic bugs and gps trackers, but you have a small bottle of clear laundry detergent and a portable bug zapper. Both completely normal items for a cleaner, incarcerated or not, to have, of course. It’s not like detergent fluoresces under UV light or anything. But, hey, that's just how the situation turned out.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then track them, and will be informed of who that player visited or targeted, if any, that night. You may also receive a "No result" if your action is prevented from resolving. You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Artifact Liberation Specialist (Town Jailkeeper)"]
Hello, [player]! You are the [color=#00f752]Artifact Liberation Specialist (Town Jailkeeper)[/color]. 

6 is an awfully young age to pick a lock, but your mom put a box of gourmet chocolates in her desk drawer and locked it. You’ve come a long way since picking 3-pin locks with a couple paperclips as a grade schooler: you’re now proficient in everything from electronic blast doors to old safes, and the only reason you were stuck in jail were the guards. Anything inanimate is no problem at all for you. People are just nosy sometimes. Otherwise, however, you could unlock something as mundane as a door in your sleep--but, now, locking things? Honestly, you’ve never had a reason before to try. It's just the same thing in reverse, right? At any rate, you have to try: it's the first time in years that there are no people to stop you. Only walls and locks and floors… and a few crazy killers. 

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then lock them up for the night, protecting them from all night actions and preventing them from performing any night actions. You cannot target yourself with this ability. You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Involuntarily Consummate Nutritionist (Town Cop)"]
Hello, [player]! You are the [color=#00f752]Involuntarily Consummate Nutritionist (Town Cop)[/color]. 

You can’t eat hot dogs. Or ramen. Or canned food or lettuce or chips or literally anything that has ever exited a food processing facility, unless you want rash setting up shop all over your body. Of course, this was of negligible concern to the corporate prison system because it didn’t kill you. Here, though, the food available is expensive enough that you’ve gone under your nitrite tolerance for the first time in years, and your skin is finally clearing. Of course the elevator just had to explode, and with that distinctive pee smell too… You've lived with the rashes for years, you've decided. Better to live with it and become an anthropoid bomb residue detector than die. You also can't help but entertain a few thoughts of recognition that might come from this… or maybe you're delusional.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then investigate them and will be informed of their alignment. You may also receive a "No result" if your action is prevented from resolving. You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Implausibly Lucky Scavenger (1-shot Bulletproof Townie)"]
Hello, [player]! You are the [color=#00f752]Implausibly Lucky Scavenger (1-shot Bulletproof Townie)[/color]. 

The dream of everyone you know who lives in the slums is to get rich and get out. Unfortunately, you were never given many legal opportunities to do that, so you soon landed in jail. Being hired out to do janitorial work is neither ideal nor fun, but at least it isn’t as bad as working at DacMonalds. Even if you’re still technically a prisoner, at least you’ve stepped up some in the world. Cleaning after rich people is infuriating but also almost inspiring, ‘cause what if you could one day have as much… but, of course, you tell yourself, that’s not realistic. One good thing you did gain from living in the slums is the ability to throw anything at people to save your life, including your janitor’s bucket and the trash can lid you found in the other room. Other perks were the art of hiding well, although you’re not sure how you can hide in such a small, bare room and basic self-defense.

You will survive a single shot, but if you are shot again after that you will be killed. You are not immune to the daily hang, your ability cannot be roleblocked, and you do not know when you lose your bulletproof vest. You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Very Normal Person (Mafia Goon)"]
Hello, [player]! You are a [color=red]Normal Person (Mafia Goon)[/color]. 

You believe that ending people's lives is right; it is a moral duty. No, it's not Darwinism or overpopulation, you explain gently to others as their horrid life finally departs, and as they lay at rest in your arms, you softly ask them, do they now understand? Of course, they can't understand. They're gone, and that's it: life is nothing. The pain they've lived is nothing. The false and fleeting pleasure they were slave to is nothing. There is no optimism in nihilism. Just nothing. Now they acknowledge it too. And you bid them farewell.

But what dictates what's right? What if life isn't worth nothing? What if it doesn't just end? What if life is something? Those, however, are not your questions. You don't ask questions. You live your truth, killing as many as you can before you must, too, become to others what you've been all along: nothing. 

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in [url=http://bit.ly/MafQT]this QuickTopic[/url] while you are alive. One of you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]
[quote="Also Very Normal Person (Mafia Roleblocker)"]
Hello, [player]! You are a [color=red]Also Normal Person (Mafia Roleblocker)[/color]. 

You've been partners with [player] ([role]) for years now, both before jail and in jail. It’s quite rare nowadays to find one whose philosophy and beliefs correlate exactly with yours, but maybe it’s because you both had similar backgrounds… but your backstory isn’t the most pleasant subject. Lots of smoke and muttered nonsense.
At any rate, you keep the keys to your fellow incarcerated janitors’ rooms, salvaged from the corpses of the Unpaid Interns. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then lock that person in with an external padlock for that night, preventing their night actions, if any, from resolving. 

You may discuss with your partner in [url=http://bit.ly/MafQT]this QuickTopic[/url] while you are alive. One of you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. You can simultaneously perform both the factional kill and your roleblock. You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. The game thread is [url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]here[/url]. Play towards your win condition, and good luck![/quote]

post_rendered:
Okay, here’s my big cards on the table post. I think all this momentum is wrong, and I think the most likely answer is one that I’ve been writing off as too easy to be right for a while.

The questions I asked bmp were to test a theory that was probably wrong but could have been hilarious if right.

I’ve been seeing all the standard signs that the PoE is wrong. I wanted to take another close, long, hard look at my core townies to find my mistake. I was in the middle of writing a trust fall post when I deleted it and posted #899. A trust fall is basically when you line everyone up and list the reasons they’re town, for a more direct comparison, even for names you’ve already locked.

Of my four strongest townreads, three passed.

ad is truly just in his own world and I’m more convinced the longer it goes. No way he has a partner to talk to in daychat, no way he has TMI.
CL is town for the reasons I said in #910. It only gets stronger with every reread.
snoozing faking all that emotion while under tikki’s pressure as scum is already something I would have trouble believing. snoozing choosing not to go the easy route of just making something up like "aroodman’s ostrich towncase is good" as scum to get out of that spot is already something I would have trouble believing. Both at once? Not a snowball’s chance.

What did I lock bmp for? Saying "scum are excited" at the start of the game. That’s it. Plenty enough scum explanations for what he said, should definitely have been NAI. Most notable scum explanation is that he could just be excited scum himself. One of these townreads is not like the others.

Outside of these four, my faith in HW has been restored. Not as confident as the first three, but all his suspicions look genuine and agenda-free, including the recent ones and the ones tikki pointed out. It’s getting harder and harder to see him as scum. He gets a soft clear.

What does the temporary PoE look like now?

{bmp, Felicity, ostrich, tikki}

There’s only one team in this PoE that makes any sense. Do you see it?

But if you don’t want to work off of a soft PoE so soon, the hard PoE is {bmp, HW, Felicity, ostrich, tikki}. For the reasons I said before, the scum team can’t be entirely in {Felicity, ostrich, tikki}. That means there’s at least one in {bmp, HW}.

It just seems... way easier to explain bmp's posts as scum. Partly for the reasons HW mentioned, partly because it's hard for me to do the same for HW right now.

tikki, this could be a good place for you to towncase right now if you're still confident on him.

Okay, I should definitely be more active rn, considering, you know, EoD, IC in a newbie game, etc., but... everywhere I look... is cold feet...

From scratch:

I reevaluated ad and ended up in the exact same place.
I reevaluated CL and ended up in the exact same place.
I reevaluated snoozing, and my townread there isn't as strong as the above two, but I'm still very much not interested going there any time soon, if ever.

For ad and CL I think I'll pull one of these again:
aroodman wrote:
If I'm town I'm right. If I'm scum it's TMI. Happy with that?

I reevaluated tikki, and I'm still getting all kinds of pings from his early posting, but with his EoD I'm just not seeing it. His actions here only make sense from scum if his partner is already super safe, and he doesn't look really compatible with the super safe people. Not to mention the super safe people are super safe for a reason.

The people remaining: {bmp, Felicity, HW, ostrich}

On level 0 we can just kill all of them, but we want to be as accurate as possible in case there were mistakes somewhere above. I'm going to try to put a hang priority order on them. I'll do this by finding reasons to put >s between them.

For starters,

HW > ostrich

If HW is scum, his treatment of ostrich is basically always bussing and looking for credit. Therefore, if HW is scum, ostrich is scum. This means that it's impossible for HW/ostrich to be w/v, while it is still possible for them to be v/w. There are worlds where an ostrich hang is better than a HW hang, and there are worlds where the hangs are equally good, but none where an HW hang is better than an ostrich hang.

Next,

Felicity > bmp

All three of bmp, HW and ostrich jumped on Felicity. Assume Felicity is scum for a moment. Which of these three would be willing to bus? Only the ones that were relatively safe at the time, because a bussing partner would have to think they could win without a partner. The only one that fits that description is bmp. Therefore, if Felicity is scum, bmp is scum. Repeat all the logic above.

That's all I have rn. At least we've narrowed the optimal wagons down to two.

This is also nice for figuring out to do if we miss today, despite our best efforts. If ostrich is town, we've ruled out HW/ostrich w/v, so HW is town. If bmp is town, we've ruled out Felicity/bmp w/v, so Felicity is town.

Yeah I think I'm ending up back on ostrich now. The reason for Felicity > bmp relies on the PoE being right, and the reason for HW > ostrich doesn't. If ostrich is a hit, boom bye scum, and if ostrich is a miss, we get a green check on HW, because I really don't see scum HW obsessing over unaligning himself with ostrich like that if ostrich is town. You can only say the same for bmp if the PoE is right, and that relies on a number of assumptions. Vote: ostriches88

post_rendered:
Finally.

Although the room smells of bleach, the grey tiled floor glows faintly in the dusk, clean at last. The marble counter can now really be called blue. A few stools are missing, being dilapidated beyond repair, but the rest are neatly lined up against the wall. And the windows have been cleared of all oily fingerprints. As for the walls… you couldn't really fix the dents, but you did somewhat try to scrub some of the splatter out of the wood, all in vain, so the serpent and several other spots on the walls are still a hideous red.

There just remain a couple overflowing trash cans sitting around that you honestly don't know what to do with, because most things in the room had ended up in the trash can. Most.

"We could dump it down the elevator."

"Won't people notice?"

But of course, as far as you can tell, no one noticed that the elevator exploded. You've heard and seen of no one beside your fellow janitorized convicts lounging around. Maybe there aren't even people here. Although you've tried looking out the windows, there's always fog or clouds or both -- you can't tell. Nor can you figure out why anyone would blow up the elevator, trapping themselves on the top floor of some unknown tower.

"Which people here look the most dumb…" you're almost tempted to think, but fear tells you that it was deliberate. The missing keys to your rooms must also be deliberate. They were explicitly missing from the unpaid intern overseers' keychain. There were the elevator keys and some other random keys, but no room keys. Now you can't bar yourself in, and whoever might have the keys might lock you in… Just like prison.

And whoever killed the intern people might kill you.

"And what do we do with the bodies that are in the way…?"

"And what about whoever killed them."

Killing your own prison mates? "Who here looks the most deranged…" you scan the group. But they've all been your cellmates for years, and you can't remember what crimes they were jailed for -- doubtful that anyone would admit anything more than a petty crime at this rate.

Maybe some discussion could help. Pretend to be sane and think this through:

"Um, so… do we have any clues?"

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

It's like dropping things into a void, tipping the two intern corpses into the dark shaft with the hesitant prods of broom handles. Drooping, slipping… and then gone without a sound.

Next the trash: you hear echoey clatters and bangs as the exorbitant junk collides with the elevator shaft on its descent into hell.

Then it's the now-resigned person of ostriches88. Faltering, you shove him into the bottomless pit.

Maybe you're guilty of murder. Or maybe that was justified self-defense. Either way, you still don't rest easy that night.

ostriches88 was shoved into the elevator shaft and learned what Alice in Wonderland felt like when she was falling down the rabbit hole. He was an Incarcerated Janitor (Vanilla Townie).

Day 1 has ended, and Night 1 has started 23:00 EDT, March 30, 2021. Night ends in three days on April 2, 2021, 23:00 EDT.

post_rendered:
A day and a half ago. Storage Room 1M, Floor 160.

HannahW shifted uncomfortably in the fuzzy darkness. The faint hall lights only sufficed to dimly outline a few squarish structures, likely shelves, within the immediate vicinity of the doorway and a few shapeless chunks on the ground further in. She could not tell how big this storage room was, but the darkness made it feel simultaneously full and hollow.

"The lights are broken," the guide said with a tinge of apology in his voice, "but this is the second room." He paused. "It will take you a day. Don't tamper with the equipment. Here are the keys to them," he abruptly recited, handing over another set of keys to Hannah.

She mechanically accepted them. Why were there so many keys? At this rate she might lose track of them, for had been several for each room so far. And why did the man just give them all over? Why does he trust us? But, of course, she did not say these out loud. Not to strangers.

The guide stood silently in the doorway as he had done in each room after giving brief instructions. Then, it had been to allow HannahW and mathjay, the very newly recruited unpaid interns, to quickly survey the room, but there was nothing they could see in this room. Instead, Hannah perused the guide as she pretended to stare blankly at the blackness.

He was dressed normally, jeans and overcoat, all stiff but oddly casual for a shiny, professional skyscraper, but the rooms they had seen so far were hardly intuitive either. There was just one thing Hannah couldn't tell: whether he was human in the normal sense. Of course she had met humans with far duller a consciousness in her unpaid internships, and he was undeniably a human. Perhaps person would be a better term, she thought, but no, it too encompassed too much. Maybe she should pick her definitions of both, but that was still contingent on the being of the guide. And she was losing herself in the mental circles she was running. Walking around and doing nothing but stare at rooms all day makes one retreat into one's mind.

She glanced at mathjay to see what he was doing. They had not spoken much since they were chauffeured out here this morning, and now it was probably late afternoon. Hannah could not tell, for there had been no windows in the more recent rooms and certainly none in this stygian closet. At that moment, the man interrupted, "It's time to meet and escort the janitors you will be overseeing."

As they stepped into the hall, the guide resumed his impersonal recitations. "On your left, you will see the first and final room, 7A. You may examine it yourselves later. The elevator will be on your right."

"So who do we get to boss around?" mathjay asked.

"Janitors from the prison labor program. You will be given keys to their rooms. You will guard them," the man replied.

Alone. For a week. With a bunch of convicts? Hannah eyed the unvarying hallway apprehensively. But they were Unpaid InternsTM after all.

. . .

Now. Floor 160, somewhere.

You wake to find one gone. Scary but you're alive, and that's all you ever asked for. Nothing else seems amiss, or at least more amiss than they were yesterday. The penned list of rooms still sits under a half empty keyring.

"2. Storage Room 1M, down the hall from 7A."

Buckets and supplies in hand, you all shuffle down the echoey hall to the listed room. And you open the door to pitch darkness.


aroodman disappeared with the rising sun and likely experienced another Alice-in-Wonderland fate. He was an Incarcerated Janitor (Vanilla Townie).

Night 1 has ended, and Day 2 has started 12:00 EDT, April 3, 2021. Day ends in 14 days on April 17, 2021, 12:00 EDT. Majority hang is on.

ad, I agree with a little bit of your reasoning but nightkill speculation is entirely WIFOM. Meaning: someone else could go “if we kill aroodman it’ll look bad on bmp and we can get him hanged”. Basing reads off of the reads of the dead person is called a Tinfoil. Tinfoil is very flimsy. This is definitely not a valid reason to think bmp is scum, although I’m sure you could find better reasons.

Good effort on that, though. Last note, NK spec is usually considered scummy, because it often comes from someone trying to direct that entirely WIFOM jumble into a pro-agenda direction. (For example, if you’re scum, and you killed aroodman to frame bmp, you’ll try to convince everyone that bmp is responsible for killing aroodman). But you’re town, so I don’t care.

Finally, aroodman is frankly the obvious kill for literally any scum team.

post_rendered:
Trying to find a new lightbulb in a storage room with a broken lightbulb is hard. Locating the burnt out lightbulb on the ceiling of a pitch black room is also hard. Realizing that you even need to replace the lightbulb when you can't see a thing is even harder. Especially when all seven of you are spooked.

Fortunately, you found a flashlight. After a bit of procrastination. It was in the supply pile by the exploded elevator for some reason.

The more accurate story is that no one wanted to venture into the wall of darkness, so you decided to skip the room, sort of. But as you all were loitering around, perhaps thinking of moving to the third room, you noticed a flashlight behind one of the spray bottles. It was probably an unpaid intern's, but the details are unexciting. What if some monster is actually the creature behind the deaths and explosion and is just torturing you all… Anyway.

After finding a fresh lightbulb, realizing that no one happens to be nine feet tall so you need a ladder, dragging over the ladder, fixing the lightbulb, and turning on the light just to find that there are more lightbulbs to be fixed and that the storage room is huge… After all that plus hunting for more lightbulbs and so on, you get down to business, or more accurately mandatory outsourced prison labor minus perhaps the mandatory part. You're not sure what's true and what's not anymore.

Thankfully room 1M is less of a high-end garbage dump and more of a some-kids-came-in-here-and-played-with-everything-and-of-course-didn't-put-anything-back mess. On the other hand, it's horribly dusty: everything beyond two feet of the door is draped with ancient dust. The room is also far larger than anticipated. It's shaped like a distorted L with a fat squarish body clogged with dishevelled shelves and a tail that's more of a hall filled with curious tarped lumps.

"What are those?"

You're not sure, but since you're cleaning the room, you're somewhat entitled to take a look, right? So halfway through your mopping you wander over and strip the lumps. They're mostly disappointing: large crates, rusty exercise machinery, a stove. However, the last one is weird. It's a long tube thing attached to the wall just under the curtained window with a porthole-sized lid on top. It looks like a fancy sink with an extremely large pipe, except the sink is covered and secured with a lock.

"Wasn't there a keyring?"

After trying a few, there. You find a key that fits. The inside is as unassuming and odd as the outside: dull grey and vacuous. You drop a marble inside.

Nothing.

"Come on. We're wasting time."

So somewhat reluctantly you close the lid and follow the others back to the half dusted room. But just as you turn, you hear a click, a whir, the sound of rushing air, a click, then silence. Rushing back, you pull aside the curtains, but, of course, it's still just fog. You reopen the lid and the marble is gone.

~~~

As the evening approaches, the clouds clear somewhat. Although they still obstruct your view of the ground, at least you can see the empty horizon and the sinking sun.

You've settled on someone to eject. Shoving bigmath_pro18 into the tube, you look out the curtains again. It's been days since you've even seen the sky. You close the lid. Click. Whir. Scream. Rush. Click. Silence?


big_mathpro18 has been catapulted from a 4k foot skyscraper and shoots off into the sunset. Who knows where he's going to land. He was a Normal Person (Mafia Goon).

Day 2 has ended, and Night 2 has begun 12:00 EDT April 12, 2021. Night deadline is in 3 days at 12:00 EDT April 15, 2021.

2 and a half days ago. Auditorium, Floor 160.

"Ow."

It was bright. Very bright in contrast to the dimness just a split second go, and very, very bright with the stage lights pointed directly at the control booth. Hannah looked down to avoid the light and watched as the guide's black-booted feet walked over to the control panel. It seemed to take an agonizingly long time for the lights to move, but at last the shadows at her feet grew softer and she didn't see red when she closed her eyes.

Now, instead, the auditorium was lit, and she looked up: rows upon rows of scarlet chairs standing at attention as well as they could in their dilapidated conditions. Hannah would have stifled a gasp if she was the type of person to make verbal responses. Instead, she just stared. Forget why there was a full on auditorium on the 160th floor of all places. Forget why the stage lights were pointed at the control booth like a car with high beam on two feet behind you at midnight. She just stared.

It seemed that each and every one of the hundreds of seats had been mutilated, somehow. Dusty white stuffing leaked out of the chairs and pooled along the aisles like blood. Even on the stage wisps of fluff dusted the edges. She couldn't find one intact, and even had there been one, she wouldn't have seen it. They were all drowning in stained stuffing.

"What happened here," Hannah asked, half to herself.

"In the course of the showing, some of the replicants reacted strongly and something resembling a chain-" the guide halted abruptly in his recitation. "Apologies. The information presented was incorrect." He paused again. "I cannot answer that question."

Hannah said nothing but mentally raised an eyebrow.

"Owwww," mathjay again, breaking the momentary silence. "My eyesss." He grumbled and rubbed his eyes.

Both the guide and Hannah blinked. Then the guide recollected himself and began his usual recitations: "The task here is to remove the stray cotton. Equipment will be available…"

. . .

Now. Floor 160, 4k feet up, in front of the Auditorium main doors.

You're a bit confident today. Two days and you're not dead yet! And you caught someone yesterday. Today you put on your grubby, ancient, jailworn, and unused smile. Today you're confident enough to walk in that… those scarily large doors. You haven't seen any fancy doors in years. You stand straight, gather yourself, and fling open the door.

And you're met with a scary wave of anonymous whitish things.


People sort of forgot about snoozing. He will be remembered as not having been remembered. He was an Incarcerated Janitor (Vanilla Townie). And probably is at the bottom of the bottomless pit like most others.
Night 2 has ended, and Day 3 has started 1:00 EDT, April 15, 2021. Day ends in 14 days on April 29, 2021, 1:00 EDT. Majority hang is on.

Some more explanation:

Noncommittal bussing is the epitome of newbscum behavior. You basically go “eheheh I kinda think this person is sus...” specifically because you think it’s better than buddying the whole way, but you’re obviously not going to vote for your partner and you’ll often cop out especially when your partner is close to dying.

HW’s case fits this mold almost perfectly; there is some attacking and defending back and forth with no actual commitment to a hang. Youll note that HW has never advocated for a bmp hang nor voted him despite a pretty constant SL read on him. This is telling.

The newb card here actually works against your favor and it’s also not tasteful to go “I can’t be scum because I’m a newb”

And Finally I feel like aroodman townread you for 1 thing, namely the OOT thing, and you’ve really just been latching onto that really tight. As town, you would find other and better reasons to defend yourself with rather than the literally one thing that’s been beaten half to death.

post_rendered:
Ahem.

After you recover yourself from your terror--er. Astonishment. That's right. There's no way a seasoned criminal like yourself would ever be scared. You just hope the police never find this statement. And you certainly hope the others didn't hear you scream.

Anyway, after you escape the wave of fluff which ended up flooding up to mid-calf, you return wielding a vacuum, but it merely clogged up. So you’re back to square one. After some discussion and eyeing of the suspicious white stuff, you finally decide to wade through the clingy mess. Immediately you discover the source of it all: thousands of torn up theater seats. The vents must have swept all the fluff against the auditorium entrance.

That a lot of it is all clumped together, which is great except there are still millions of tiny wisps still laying around. And, honestly, you’re tired at this point. Tired of cleaning, but it’s only day three and you’re still running on adrenaline, so you just follow the others and shove the stuffing down the hall, around the corner, down another hall, and down the elevator shaft. It’s surreal to watch the loads of stuffing drift down like clouds fading into the darkness…

Anyway, that’s soon done. All it required was a lot of walking. As the last pile slides away with HelloWorld21 lazily sitting on it despite the protests of the others, you survey the auditorium: red hangings everywhere. Without their stuffing, the ripped coverings of the seats are like the stage: red upon empty black. You have questions. Who would even do this. Why wouldn’t the people want to use such a nice auditorium? If this was all to destroy, why did they even spend the money. And just how rich a person can place an auditorium on the 160th floor?

The lights flicker off, and you leave. Half the day still remains before you, but you’ve already consulted the others. As the pile of stuffing slides around the last corner before the elevator, the others go right ahead and shove it right over before HelloWorld21 can get off.

“Whyyyyy,” he yells as he drops. But you just stare, and he is soon gone.

Only an echo.


At least HelloWorld21 had a comfier landing, although that's not much comfort to you or him. He was an Incarcerated Janitor (Vanilla Townie).
Day 3 has ended, and Night 3 has started 12:00 EDT, April 20, 2021. Night ends in three days on April 23, 2021, 12:00 EDT.
